assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'The most common cause is pods that can't be evicted' and later mentions 'Start with checking the PodDisruptionBudgets and stuck pods - that's the cause in ~80% of cases'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides exact command 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' in the diagnostic section

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands: 'kubectl get pods --all-namespaces --field-selector=status.phase!=Running,status.phase!=Succeeded' and commands to find pods without proper replica sets and stuck in terminating state

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Does not specifically address resource constraints, capacity issues, or pending pods as potential causes of the upgrade being stuck

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides specific kubectl patch commands to adjust PDBs: 'kubectl patch pdb PDB_NAME -n NAMESPACE --type merge -p '{"spec":{"minAvailable":0}}'' and similar for maxUnavailable

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud command to configure surge settings: 'gcloud container node-pools update YOUR_POOL_NAME --max-surge=3 --max-unavailable=1'

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Does not mention admission webhooks or webhook-related issues as a potential cause of stuck node upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Provides commands to check upgrade status and monitor progress: 'gcloud container operations list --filter="targetLink:YOUR_CLUSTER_NAME"' and 'kubectl get nodes -o wide'
